verdicts.note: The measure renews local revenue for an already-existing Tacoma service contract; it neither creates regional consolidation nor changes who governs service delivery. Continuation of the status quo supplies no direction on local-control versus regional consistency.

review_notes: Display review: all four what_it_does and cost_line fields match the dossiers, including the absence of a household estimate for Fire District No. 10. Pro and con summaries are accurately attributed: Carbonado and Fircrest rely on official explanations with no identified opposition statement; Tacoma summarizes both filed committees; Fire District No. 10 summarizes its pro committee and states that no con statement was filed. Carbonado and Fircrest tax directions of 1 correctly reflect renewals, Tacoma's tax direction of 2 reflects two reinstated taxes for ten years, and the fire-station bond's tax direction of 2 reflects new excess levies for up to 20 years. Tacoma spending 2 and fire-station spending 1 appropriately distinguish a broad transportation program from a facility-specific capital project.
